channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: New Jersey Democrat proposes bill to create travel advisories to inform pregnant women of state abortion laws
item.description: A New Jersey Democrat proposed a bill to create travel advisories to notify women of restrictive abortion laws in other states they may be visiting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In VP debate, Walz acknowledges he wasn’t in China during Tiananmen uprising
item.description: APM Reports, the investigations and documentaries unit of MPR News, first reported that Walz’s claim during a 2014 congressional hearing on the 25th anniversary of the massacre didn’t square up with media accounts in 1989.
item.dc:creator: Tom Scheck, Curtis Gilbert, Caspar von Au, and Forest Hunt, MPR News

item.title: Walz, Vance double down on dueling abortion views
item.description: November 2024 will mark the first U.S. presidential election since the Supreme Court overturned its decision in Roe v. Wade.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Harris, Trump's approach to Mideast crisis, hurricane to test public mood in final weeks of campaign
item.description: A trio of new trials — a devastating hurricane, expanding conflict in the Mideast and a dockworkers strike that threatens the U.S. economy — are looming over the final weeks of the presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Zeke Miller And Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pennsylvania town grapples with Trump assassination attempt ahead of his return
item.description: BUTLER, Pa. (AP) — Donald Trump is going back to Butler, where the world last saw him pump his fist and beseech followers to “fight,” even as blood streaked his face from a would-be assassin's bullet.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some of the most notable quotes from the JD Vance-Tim Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: The debate between Ohio Senator JD Vance and Minnesota Governor Tim Walz featured an often orderly, policy-focused and even civil dialogue between the two men seeking to serve as the next president’s second in command.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Walz repeats Georgia abortion death falsehood decried by doctors as 'fearmongering'
item.description: Tim Walz claimed during the vice presidential debate that a Georgia woman likely died due to the state's abortion laws despite doctors slamming the narrative as "fearmongering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Vance on carbon emissions and climate change: 'Let's just say that's true'
item.description: “One of the things that I've noticed some of our Democratic friends talking a lot about is a concern about carbon emissions — this idea that carbon emissions drive all the climate change,” Vance said.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: A visibly shaky Walz says the world needs 'steady leadership'
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz kicked off his debate against Ohio Sen. JD Vance on a shaky note as he answered a question regarding his foreign policy platform in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know on claims made about Minnesota, Walz in VP debate against Vance
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz’s home state came up early in his debate against fellow vice presidential candidate JD Vance on Tuesday evening. Here are the facts.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: School board to pay $575K to teacher fired for not using transgender student's pronouns
item.description: A Virginia school board has agreed to pay a former high school teacher more than $500,000 in damages and attorneys' fees as part of a legal settlement after the instructor's dismissal in 2018 for refusing to use a transgender student's pronouns.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump rallies in Wisconsin's critical Democratic stronghold ahead of the vice presidential debate
item.description: WAUNAKEE, Wis. (AP) — Former President Donald Trump spent more than an hour ahead of Tuesday night's vice presidential debate campaigning in a Democratic county that is crucial to Kamala Harris ' hopes for winning the key battleground state of Wisconsin.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump won't participate in interview for '60 Minutes' election special
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — CBS News said Tuesday that Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump has declined to participate in an interview with “60 Minutes” for its election special, which will go forward next Monday with Democratic opponent Kamala Harris alone.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NY race for US Senate: Gillibrand talks Adams indictment, term limits, economy
item.description: Come Nov. 5, the 15-year Democratic senator will defend her seat against Republican challenger Mike Sapraicone.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, NewsNation

item.title: Are convicted felons allowed to vote in Illinois?
item.description: With just five weeks until the 2024 presidential election, most U.S. citizens over the age of 18 are eligible to vote as long as they are registered. However, for some individuals, the right to vote may be more complicated due to a criminal conviction.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: Volunteers from deep blue Illinois learn to canvas in battleground states
item.description: Across the seven states likely to decide the presidential election, thousands of volunteers, including some from Illinois, are on the ground going door-to-door working to increase voter turnout.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: GOP leaders press Biden administration to get tougher on Iran after missile attack
item.description: Republican leaders on Capitol Hill are calling on President Biden to get tougher on Iran in the wake of Tuesday’s missile attack on Israel, urging the administration to adopt a series of specific measures designed to cripple the military powers of Tehran and its proxies.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump speaks in Wisconsin ahead of VP debate
item.description: The former president criticized Kamala Harris and Joe Biden for their handling of the conflict in the Middle East and the border.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Harris, Trump woo Gen Z voters in critical swing states
item.description: The Harris and Trump campaigns are stepping up their outreach to Generation Z voters in critical battlegrounds, targeting a voting bloc expected to play a key role in a tight White House race where any marginal advantage gained could make the difference.
item.dc:creator: Yash Roy, NewsNation

item.title: Unearthed photo shows Tim Walz appointee decorated house with posters of murderous communist dictators
item.description: Ida Rukavina, appointed by Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz to lead the state's Department of Iron Range Resources and Rehabilitation, posted a photo on social media in 2021 showing her walls decorated with communist imagery.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida Republican 'absolutely certain' there will be supplemental to address Helene aid
item.description: Rep. Kat Cammack (R-Fla.) said Monday she’s “absolutely certain” Congress will consider a supplemental aid package to provide additional disaster relief to areas devastated by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Gillibrand vs. Sapraicone: The Race for US Senate forum
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Democratic Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand and Republican candidate Mike Sapraicone faced off in a Senate forum that aired on PIX11 News Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, NewsNation

item.title: Walz and Vance go in depth on policy while attacking each other’s running mates in VP debate
item.description: In a debate that evoked a calmer era in American politics, Tim Walz and JD Vance on Tuesday went after each other’s running mates and sought to shore up their campaigns' vulnerabilities at a time of renewed fears of a regional war in the Middle East and sadness over devastation from Hurricane Helene
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price And Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A century of Jimmy Carter: Longest-living former president turns 100
item.description: In 1998, at the ripe age of 74, former President Carter put some of his thoughts about getting older to paper. "The virtues of aging," Carter wrote in a book that year, "include both the blessings that come to us as we grow older and what we have to offer that might be beneficial to others.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says ‘1 rough hour’ of policing would end crime
item.description: "See, we have to let the police do their job. And if they have to be extraordinarily rough—" Trump said at a rally.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Biden administration expands asylum restrictions at southern border
item.description: The Biden administration announced Monday it will expand its asylum restrictions at the U.S.-Mexico border, cementing tougher standards that will cut off access to protections for many migrants.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmakers fear 'chaos' if Trump-Harris race goes to the wire
item.description: Democratic senators say they fear Trump and his allies will seize on any initial uncertainty over the results to claim election fraud.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about the Walz-Vance debate
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) and Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) are set to go head-to-head on Tuesday night in their first and likely only debate as vice presidential contenders.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: Trump suspect pleads not guilty in attempted assassination
item.description: The man accused of attempting to assassinate former President Trump at one of his Florida golf courses earlier this month has pleaded not guilty to five federal charges, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Austrian far-right party wins national vote but its chances of governing are unclear
item.description: The Freedom Party on Sunday secured the first far-right national parliamentary election victory in post-World War II Austria. But political rivals have said they won’t work with the party’s leader.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: 5 people killed in a 4-vehicle chain reaction crash on central Utah highway
item.description: Four people from Minnesota and a Utah man were killed in a four-vehicle chain reaction crash in central Utah. The Utah Highway Patrol says an SUV pulling onto U.S. Highway 6 near Price overshot the acceleration lane and pulled into the path of a semitractor pulling double trailers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: The Pentagon says it wants to prevent Iran from spreading the conflict in the Middle East
item.description: After Israel killed Hezbollah’s leader, the U.S. says it wants to stop Iran from “exploiting the situation or expanding the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster and Tom Bowman, MPR News

item.title: What Tim Walz's past debate performances reveal about how he may take on JD Vance
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz’s track record suggests the Democratic governor will focus on his experiences pre-politics. Those who’ve debated him before say Walz has a down-to-earth style that is relatable — but that style can also cause problems for the governor.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Oregon senator introduces bill to reform Supreme Court, add 6 more seats
item.description: Oregon Sen. Ron Wyden introduced bill on Thursday that would bring fundamental changes to the nation's high court.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, Fox 5

item.title: City Council passes bill to decriminalize jaywalking: What happens next?
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Jaywalking in New York City could soon be legal. City Council passed a bill on Thursday to decriminalize it. NYPD officers issue hundreds of jaywalking tickets each year, the majority of them to people of color, city data showed.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11
